sanskrit:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन॥
transliteration:
vedāhaṃ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna |
bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṃ tu veda na kaścana ||
literal_meaning: I know the past, present and future beings, Arjuna; yet no one knows Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna asserts absolute omniscience while highlighting the universal human blind-spot: we do not truly know the source of our own awareness.  Psychologically, it mirrors the split between the ego’s story-making machinery (past/future rumination) and the un-knowable Ground of Being that silently observes it.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance—an acute felt gap between the mind’s need for control-through-knowledge and the lived reality that the ‘knower’ itself cannot be objectified.
trigger_scenario: A high-functioning professional realizes that every predictive model, life plan, or self-narrative is built on assumptions about a ‘self’ that remains mysteriously elusive under introspection.
anchor_text: The silent awareness behind every thought already knows you; relax the need to grasp it.
